channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: 'Let them shower in hotels': Johannesburg Premier faces backlash amid water crisis
item.description: In South Africa, as taps run dry in Johannesburg, Africa's richest city, a tone deaf remark by a senior politician there unleashes fury.

item.title: Russia's hybrid warfare rattles Poland and NATO
item.description: Russia is stepping up covert attacks across Europe — rail sabotage, drones, cyberstrikes — testing NATO. Polish officials warn "disposable agents" are...

item.title: Trump would like the government he leads to pay him billions
item.description: President Trump is asking the federal government for billions of dollars in damages, putting his own Justice Department on the spot and creating an un...

item.title: Do the people building the AI chatbot Claude understand what they've created?
item.description: Anthropic is one of the world's most powerful AI firms. New Yorker writer Gideon Lewis-Kraus explains how they're trying to make chatbot Claude more e...

item.title: Australia bans a citizen with alleged IS links from returning from Syria
item.description: The Australian is among a group of 34 women and children who had planned to fly from Damascus to Australia on Monday but were turned back by Syrian au...

item.title: The children of late civil rights leader Jesse Jackson honor his legacy a day after his death
item.description: Jesse Jackson’s children remember his civil rights legacy and describe him as a devoted father after his death at home.

item.title: Ilia Malinin reflects on Olympic experience: "You learn a lot more from failure"
item.description: Star figure skater Ilia Malinin stressed he was focused on moving forward and continuing to push the boundaries of the sport.

item.title: Trump hosts Black History Month event at White House
item.description: President Trump held an event at the White House on Wednesday honoring Black History Month. Mr. Trump expressed his condolences to the family of the R...

item.title: Trump administration appeals judge's order to restore slavery exhibits
item.description: The Trump administration filed an appeal after a judge ordered slavery exhibits that were removed from the President's House Site to be returned.

item.title: Jesse Jackson’s 1988 presidential run inspired generations to carry his message
What qualifies as a hardship with the IRS?
item.description: Struggling to pay your tax bill? The IRS has programs that may help, but you'll need to prove that you qualify.

item.title: Billionaire Lex Wexner tells US lawmakers he was 'naive' and 'conned' by Epstein
item.description: Testifying before Congress, the former CEO of Victoria's Secret lingerie brand accused Epstein of stealing "vast sums" of money from his family.

item.title: Zuckerberg defends Meta in landmark social media addiction trial
item.description: The billionaire chief executive faces is questioned over whether use of Instagram harms children.

item.title: Iran security official appears to fire on crowd at cemetery
item.description: It happened as people gathered to commemorate those killed during the government’s brutal crackdown on protesters

item.title: 'Difficult' Russia-Ukraine peace talks end without breakthrough
item.description: The second day of negotiations between Ukraine and Russia ended after just two hours.

item.title: Reese's family member criticizes Hershey for "quietly replacing" ingredients
item.description: Brad Reese claims Hershey is cutting costs by relying on cheaper ingredients, risking the Reese's brand.

item.title: Late-night host Stephen Colbert isn’t backing down from public dispute with CBS bosses
Do not give away Diego Garcia, Trump tells UK in fresh attack on Chagos deal
item.description: The president's comments come just a day after the US gave its official backing to the UK's Chagos deal.
